subject: [PATCH v2 3/3] arm64: escalate smp_send_stop() to an SDEI NMI as a last resort

reviews.author: Doug Anderson
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Doug Anderson pointed out that the SDEI event is never completed when a CPU is wedged, which could lead to issues with EL3 retaining the dispatch slot until reset.

Reviewer Doug Anderson requested that the patch include comments explaining the purpose and behavior of the new SDEI NMI escalation mechanism, to avoid confusion for future maintainers.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi,

On Thu, Jun 11, 2026 at 10:47 AM Kiryl Shutsemau <kirill@shutemov.name> wrote:

---

Ah, OK. This makes sense. I read the cover letter, but I guess this
part of it didn't stick in my mind. I wouldn't mind an explanation of
what's going on being included as a comment in the code. Then someone
down the line won't be left wondering.

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch adds an additional escalation path to the smp_send_stop() function on arm64 architecture, which is used to stop a CPU that has become wedged and is ignoring interrupts. When the regular IPI (Inter-Processor Interrupt) and pseudo-NMI paths fail, the patch triggers an SDEI (System Description Event Interface) NMI (Non-Maskable Interrupt) as a last resort. This allows the system to recover from the wedged CPU by sending it an SDEI event that will eventually lead to its shutdown. The patch also introduces new functions and modifications to existing code to support this escalation path.
